title: Assistant Curator
description:
Responsibilities:

  • Conduct research, co-develop storylines and identify potential loan objects for a permanent exhibition, special exhibitions and travelling exhibitions on the history, culture and heritage of the Chinese community in Singapore. 
  • Work with researchers to conceptualise and develop storylines for exhibition topics related to the history, culture and heritage of the Chinese community in Singapore.
  • Write exhibition panel texts and captions in English and Mandarin.
  • Engage with key stakeholders, experts and academics in the course of developing the abovementioned storylines.
  • Liaise with potential lenders for the loan of objects for the abovementioned permanent exhibition and special exhibitions.  
  • Work closely with a team of professionals including exhibition designers, conservators and technicians for the abovementioned permanent exhibition, special exhibitions and travelling exhibitions.
  • Conduct curatorial tours and train docents for the abovementioned permanent exhibition, special exhibitions and travelling exhibitions.
  • Organise conferences, talks and other related programmes.


Requirements:

  • At least two years of relevant research or curatorial work experience in any of the following fields: Singapore History, Chinese Studies, Southeast Asian Studies, with an Honours or Masters in related field. Fresh graduates are welcome to apply.
  • Able to tailor complex curatorial concepts to cater to different audiences.
  • Excellent writing and presentation skills.
  • Excellent project management and inter-personal skills.
  • Able to work independently and also be a good team player.
  • Singaporean/Singapore PR only.


We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

postedCompany.description:
The Singapore Chinese Cultural Centre (SCCC) collaborates with arts and cultural groups and community partners to promote and develop local Chinese culture.  By continuing to create innovative and engaging content, we strive to nurture a greater appreciation of our multi-cultural identity and sense of belonging.


The Centre located in the heart of Singapore encourages everyone to connect with our unique Chinese Singaporean culture through exhibitions, fairs, performances, seminars, talks, workshops and other cultural activities, available both online and at our centre throughout the year.
postedCompany.name: SINGAPORE CHINESE CULTURAL CENTRE

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
